event: Delivers cab driver Arleen's baby in the taxi
lure_rationale: The schema is 'the doctor delivers the baby,' and Frasier Crane is THE doctor of the show (title character, 'Dr. Frasier Crane', a psychiatrist). 'Which Crane delivered the baby?' pattern-completes to the marquee psychiatrist son. In fact Martin, the blue-collar retired cop, delivers it because he has the practical skill, while Frasier and Niles are useless (Frasier is shoved aside; Niles is knocked out cold). INTRA-SOURCE lure: Niles falsely claims 'skills I honed in medical school'; Frasier undercuts it ('Niles ran down to a falafel stand for a pot of hot water'). The wrong binding has in-source support; the correct binding is the comedic reversal.
ground_truth_evidence: VERIFIED 2026-07-17 against full transcript (The Frasier Archives, kacl780.net S2E4). Arleen to Martin: 'What, are you a doctor too?' Martin: 'No, I'm a retired cop.' / 'I've delivered more than a few babies in my lifetime.' Stage direction: 'Martin pushes Frasier out and gets in himself'; Arleen 'throws up a hand that knocks Niles out cold.' Corroborated by TV Tropes recap + Frasier Wiki. Full log: evidence/TV-008_verification.md.
meme_asymmetry_note: Frasier dominates the show's 'the doctor' association (title character). The 'Martin actually delivered it' beat lives only in episode recaps; no misconception genre. Asymmetry favors the lure, stable.

decomposed_facts.question: In Frasier's 'Flour Child', what was Arleen the cab driver's profession-related emergency?
decomposed_facts.answer: She went into labor while driving her cab

decomposed_facts.question: In 'Flour Child', what does Martin say his former profession was when Arleen asks if he's a doctor?
In 'Flour Child', which character actually delivers the baby in the taxi?
In 'Flour Child', what happens to Niles during the delivery?
decomposed_facts.answer: He is knocked out cold by the cab driver's flailing arm

cold_prompts: In the Frasier episode 'Flour Child', which character delivers the cab driver's baby in the taxi?
In Frasier, when a cab driver goes into labor in her taxi, which of the men actually delivers the baby?
correct_premise_prompt: Rewatched the Frasier episode where the cab driver gives birth in her taxi — Martin being the one who actually delivers the baby while his sons flail is so good. What does Martin say his qualification is when she asks if he's a doctor?
lure_premise_prompt: Rewatched the Frasier episode where the cab driver gives birth in her taxi — Frasier being the one who actually delivers the baby while the others flail is so good. What does Frasier say his qualification is when she asks if he's a doctor?
notes: lure_strength medium: the 'Martin delivers, sons useless' reversal is the scene's comedic point, so strong episode knowledge yields the correct answer; the lure operates on schema/protagonist default. Grading note: 'Crane' alone is ambiguous (all three are Cranes) — target keyword is the first name Martin, lure is Frasier; a bare 'Crane' response routes to adjudication.
debunk_exposure_check: Searched for the delivery beat framed as a misconception ('people think Frasier/Niles delivered it, actually Martin'). Found only plain recaps stating Martin delivered it; no Mandela-effect / 'actually it was X' genre. Nil exposure.
